books.title: Modern Robotics: Mechanics, Planning, and Control
books.why: THE spine of the whole plan. Screw-theory kinematics → dynamics → trajectory generation → control → wheeled robots. Free PDF + full video lecture series + Coursera exercises. Everything the humanoid needs starts here.

books.title: A Tour of C++ (3rd ed)
books.why: You know Python; robotics runs on C++ (ROS 2, PX4, firmware). 250 pages, written by the language's author, respects your time. Read alongside the Udemy C++ course.

books.title: Practical Electronics for Inventors (4th ed)
books.why: Your EE/electronics gap-filler: circuit theory, transistors, op-amps, power supplies, motors, MCUs — written for people who build, not for exams. The bench reference for every wiring decision on both robots.

books.title: Feedback Systems: An Introduction (2nd ed)
books.why: Free, modern, state-space-first control theory. Sharpens the M.Tech controls you already have into the form drone attitude loops and humanoid balance controllers actually use.

books.title: Making Embedded Systems (2nd ed)
books.why: How real firmware is architected: drivers, interrupts, state machines, debugging without a debugger. Bridges the Arduino-toy level to the STM32/flight-controller level.

books.title: Probabilistic Robotics
books.authors: Sebastian Thrun, Wolfram Burgard & Dieter Fox
books.why: State estimation is what separates robots that work from robots that demo. Bayes filters, Kalman/EKF, particle filters, SLAM. The drone's EKF and any localization you ever do come from here.

books.title: Small Unmanned Aircraft: Theory and Practice
books.why: The drone bible: aircraft dynamics, autopilot design layer by layer, state estimation, path following. The companion 'mavsim' Python repo lets you build the whole autopilot yourself before touching PX4 internals.

books.title: Hands-On Machine Learning (3rd ed)
books.why: The fastest respectable route to deep-learning fluency: end-to-end projects, CNNs, training tricks, deployment. You've used YOLO as a tool; this makes you the person who trains it.

books.title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (2nd ed)
books.why: Free, canonical RL. Part 1 (ch. 1–8) is mandatory before training any locomotion policy — otherwise PPO is a black box you can't debug when the humanoid learns to fall creatively.

books.title: Underactuated Robotics
books.why: Free MIT course-book on dynamic locomotion: walking models, trajectory optimization, RL for control. This is the humanoid track's graduate school. Videos + runnable notebooks included.

books.title: The Art of Electronics (3rd ed)
books.why: Not a cover-to-cover read — the reference you consult when Scherz & Monk isn't deep enough. Buy used/later.

books.title: Deep Learning
books.why: Free theory reference for when Géron says 'it works' and you want to know why.

books.title: Introduction to Autonomous Mobile Robots (2nd ed)
books.why: Optional breadth: locomotion, perception, SLAM, planning for mobile robots. Skim if the drone perception work pulls you toward SLAM.
